---
title: "Roadmaps"
page-layout: full
toc: false
number-sections: false
---

## Purpose

The roadmaps help you move through the site in dependency-aware order instead of guessing what to study next.

## Available Roadmaps

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
### Core Foundation

Use this when the question is simply where to begin and in what order to repair the basics.

[Open Core Foundation](core-foundation.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Theory Reading Track

Use this when the real goal is surviving theorem-heavy papers rather than surveying everything.

[Open Theory Reading Track](theory-reading.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### AI / ML Theory

Use this when you want the cleanest route from foundations into learning theory, high-dimensional tools, and modern ML math.

[Open AI / ML Theory](ai-ml-theory.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Engineering Systems

Use this when you care about dynamical systems, numerics, sensing, control, and systems-facing modeling.

[Open Engineering Systems](engineering-systems.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::
:::

## When To Use A Roadmap Instead Of A Topic Page

Use a roadmap when the blocker is `sequencing`.

Use a topic page when the blocker is `understanding the object itself`.
